channel.title: Performance on Aman Goyal
channel.description: Recent content in Performance on Aman Goyal

item.title: The Scatter/Gather Pattern: Scaling Computation Speed
item.description: Core Concept Scatter/Gather = parallel processing pattern A request is scattered to multiple nodes and the partial results are gathered and combined into one response Goal: Reduce latency (faster response times) When to Use Best for “embarrassingly parallel” problems that are independent and parallelizable, such as search queries, data aggregation, and analytics Key Challenges Diminishing returns: Network and coordination costs eventually outweigh the benefits of adding more nodes Straggler problem: The system speed is dictated by the slowest node; more nodes increase the chance of a slowdown Reliability: If one node fails, the entire request may fail Solutions Replicate each shard: Use multiple replicas for each shard to provide fault tolerance and load balancing Smart scaling: Balance parallelism against coordination overhead Trade-offs Pros: Faster response times and efficient handling of compute-heavy tasks Cons: Complex coordination, network overhead, and sensitivity to “tail latency” (slowest components) One-line Summary Scatter/Gather distributes a request across multiple nodes for parallel processing and combines results to reduce latency.
